essence: Inspects fire‑resistant walls, floors, ceilings, and penetrations to ensure buildings prevent the spread of fire and smoke.

identity.title: Fire Compartmentation Inspector
identity.summary: Assesses fire‑stopping, cavity barriers, penetrations, and structural fire‑resistance measures to ensure buildings meet safety and compliance standards.

salary.notes: Senior inspectors or those specialising in complex commercial or high‑rise buildings can exceed £55k-£65k+.

qualifications: Knowledge of fire‑resistance standards and building regulations
Ability to assess penetrations, barriers, and structural fire protection
Understanding of construction methods and materials

lifestyle.description: Fire compartmentation inspectors examine buildings to ensure fire‑resistant walls, floors, ceilings, and penetrations are correctly installed and maintained. They check fire‑stopping, cavity barriers, ductwork, risers, and structural protection. The role suits individuals who enjoy detailed inspection work, construction knowledge, and contributing to life‑safety compliance.

habits: Follows strict compliance and reporting standards
Keeps detailed photographic and written records
Communicates clearly with contractors and building managers
Identifies installation faults and safety risks
